message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): empty-body POST Content-Length: 0 contract (tick 326)

Added `distributed_http_client_post_empty_body_sends_content_length_zero`
— pins that POST with an empty `Vec<u8>` body sends
`Content-Length: 0` and zero body bytes after the header separator.

Why this matters: empty-body POST is common (health pings, webhooks,
idempotent-create APIs). Servers need `Content-Length: 0` to know the
request has no body — otherwise they either hang waiting or 400 the
request as malformed. A regression that stripped the header or sent
the wrong value would silently break body-less POST callers.

Test asserts three invariants on the wire:
1. The request contains `Content-Length: 0` (case-insensitive).
2. After the `\r\n\r\n` separator, there are zero body bytes.
3. Happy-path roundtrip: client sees the server's 200 OK.

Uses `req.find("\r\n\r\n")` to split headers from body, then asserts the
trailing slice is all zero-valued bytes (our read buffer is zero-init,
so any non-zero bytes after the separator mean the client wrote body
data it shouldn't have).

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 28s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
